Outlet Name: Happy Hour with Kenny Roda - WKNR-AM
Contact Subjects: Entertainment (General); Sports (General)
Contact Profile: Kenny Roda has become Cleveland's "Sports Authority" in the last decade."The Roadman" is a 4-time Cleveland A.I.R. awards winner representing excellence in Cleveland radio. He twice was named Cleveland's Best Talk Show Host, once as the Best Afternoon Drive Time Host and the other A.I.R. award was for his exclusive one-on-one interview with Tiger Woods in 2000. During his last 10 years in sports talk radio, Roda has worked his way up the ladder from the early days as "The 20/20 Ticker Dude" to his current slot as the Afternoon Drive Time Host, better known as "Happy Hour" with "The Roadman" every Monday through Friday from 3pm-7pm. Roda, a 1986 graduate of Baldwin-Wallace College, has also worked in TV with then SportsChannel Ohio and now Fox Sports Net Ohio. He served as the Cleveland Lumberjacks rink side reporter during their telecasts as well as the studio host with Michael Reghi for the pre-game and post-game shows covering the Indians during the 1995 World Series. Roda's affiliation with the Tribe is a strong one, having hosted Tribe Talk for the Indians radio network for over 3 years as well as hosting the Tribe's pre-game show for their game broadcasts. Roda also was the host for "10th Inning", a call-in show after Tribe games. The Roadman's knowledge doesn't end with baseball though. He covers the Browns on a weekly basis during the season. The only talk show host in C-Town to have been at every home game to get after game interviews since the Browns returned to the NFL in 1999. An avid basketball fan, Roda admits, as painful as it is he watches all Cavs games and follows them religiously. He also provides play-by-play and color commentary for the MAC basketball tournament every March. His combination of sports knowledge, distinctive interviewing skills, entertainment value and love for "The Hotties" has made his show, "Happy Hour", one of the top afternoon drive sports talk shows in the country.

Contact Profile: Kevin came to WTAM in March of 2000, having previous broadcast experience at the Jim Rome radio station and at WOBL in Oberlin. Sports is his expertise, but don't be surprised if you tune in and hear him "going off" on just about anything. His style is passion and energy and as he always says, "Whatever makes you think and feel is what I'm interested in. My job is to spark good conversation."

Contact Profile: Greg is the lead reporter for WTAM. From wading through ankle deep water while covering a massive water main break in downtown Cleveland to keeping our listeners informed on the corruption trial of Rep. James Traficant to stepping through the debris of devastating tornado, Greg Saber has done it all. The native Clevelander graduated from Kent State University with degrees in Communications and Political Science. Before joining the news staff at WTAM 1100, Greg worked as a reporter at WAKR-AM and WAKC-TV, both in Akron. Greg finds working in the news business a day-to-day educational experience. His hobbies include amateur radio, jogging in all kinds of weather, American politics, video production and trying to figure-out whether he needs HBO on his cable bill. His most memorable broadcast moments include forgetting what city he was in while doing his first newscast in Akron, dropping his glasses from the Goodyear Blimp and interviewing people at Hopkins Airport who had just flown over the burning World Trade Center in New York. Greg has been given Best Reporter awards from both the Ohio Associated Press and the Society of Professional Journalists
Outlet Profile: EDITORIAL PROFILE WTAM is a News, Talk and Sports channel and is home of Cleveland Indians broadcasts.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Blinn hosts "TechByter," a weekly review of current topics for Windows and Mac users. He mainly covers graphics, publishing and productivity. "Provide a quick pitch that clearly covers the basics and send only pitches that match my needs," he says. When pitching, send a list of products and topics; include a paragraph or two about each item along with company contact info. He doesn't do cookbooks, kids, wine tasting or political books. "I have a kill file for those who repeatedly send things I don't want even after I've advised them what I do want," he says. "A useful approach is a message in advance asking if I'd like to take a look at a particular piece of software or hardware and then sending it only when I have both an interest and time available." Contact by email.

Contact Profile: Judy Thompson has been anchoring the Saturday afternoon news shift since 1996. A practicing attorney, she also works on in-depth news reports concerning legal matters. Contact Profile: Greg is the lead reporter for WTAM. From wading through ankle deep water while covering a massive water main break in downtown Cleveland to keeping our listeners informed on the corruption trial of Rep. James Traficant to stepping through the debris of devastating tornado, Greg Saber has done it all. The native Clevelander graduated from Kent State University with degrees in Communications and Political Science. Before joining the news staff at WTAM 1100, Greg worked as a reporter at WAKR-AM and WAKC-TV, both in Akron. Greg finds working in the news business a day-to-day educational experience. His hobbies include amateur radio, jogging in all kinds of weather, American politics, video production and trying to figure-out whether he needs HBO on his cable bill. His most memorable broadcast moments include forgetting what city he was in while doing his first newscast in Akron, dropping his glasses from the Goodyear Blimp and interviewing people at Hopkins Airport who had just flown over the burning World Trade Center in New York. Greg has been given Best Reporter awards from both the Ohio Associated Press and the Society of Professional Journalists

Contact Profile: Kenny Roda has become Cleveland's "Sports Authority" in the last decade."The Roadman" is a 4-time Cleveland A.I.R. awards winner representing excellence in Cleveland radio. He twice was named Cleveland's Best Talk Show Host, once as the Best Afternoon Drive Time Host and the other A.I.R. award was for his exclusive one-on-one interview with Tiger Woods in 2000. During his last 10 years in sports talk radio, Roda has worked his way up the ladder from the early days as "The 20/20 Ticker Dude" to his current slot as the Afternoon Drive Time Host, better known as "Happy Hour" with "The Roadman" every Monday through Friday from 3pm-7pm. Roda, a 1986 graduate of Baldwin-Wallace College, has also worked in TV with then SportsChannel Ohio and now Fox Sports Net Ohio. He served as the Cleveland Lumberjacks rink side reporter during their telecasts as well as the studio host with Michael Reghi for the pre-game and post-game shows covering the Indians during the 1995 World Series. Roda's affiliation with the Tribe is a strong one, having hosted Tribe Talk for the Indians radio network for over 3 years as well as hosting the Tribe's pre-game show for their game broadcasts. Roda also was the host for "10th Inning", a call-in show after Tribe games. The Roadman's knowledge doesn't end with baseball though. He covers the Browns on a weekly basis during the season. The only talk show host in C-Town to have been at every home game to get after game interviews since the Browns returned to the NFL in 1999. An avid basketball fan, Roda admits, as painful as it is he watches all Cavs games and follows them religiously. He also provides play-by-play and color commentary for the MAC basketball tournament every March. His combination of sports knowledge, distinctive interviewing skills, entertainment value and love for "The Hotties" has made his show, "Happy Hour", one of the top afternoon drive sports talk shows in the country.

Contact Profile: Judy Thompson has been anchoring the Saturday afternoon news shift since 1996. A practicing attorney, she also works on in-depth news reports concerning legal matters. Judy is a veteran of the Cleveland broadcasting scene, and has worked for WTAM since 1992 as a part-time reporter and anchor. Judy is a sole practitioner with emphasis on juvenile law, criminal defense, family law and probate. She is a member of the Cuyahoga County Bar Association's Guardian Ad Litem Project, and represents children in abuse and neglect cases. A native Clevelander, she is a graduate of Villa Angela Academy, John Carroll University, where she earned a B.A. in Communications and Cleveland State University, where she received her law degree.

Outlet Profile: EDITORIAL PROFILE WBKC Radio first went on the air as WPVL on April 25, 1956 as a daytime only station and was later granted a twenty four hour license. The station is located in the Cleveland Metro Arbitron market and is physically located in Lake County, about twenty miles from downtown Cleveland. WBKC airs a three hour news magazine program between 6am and 9am Monday through Friday, and simulcasts WCLV's classical format for much of the day. WBKC is a Cleveland Browns, Cleveland Indians and Ohio State Buckeye Radio affiliate. We can be reached at 800-422-1460, by email at wbkc@wbkc.com, or at One Radio Place, Painesville, Ohio 44077.

Contact Profile: Kenny Roda has become Cleveland's "Sports Authority" in the last decade."The Roadman" is a 4-time Cleveland A.I.R. awards winner representing excellence in Cleveland radio. He twice was named Cleveland's Best Talk Show Host, once as the Best Afternoon Drive Time Host and the other A.I.R. award was for his exclusive one-on-one interview with Tiger Woods in 2000. During his last 10 years in sports talk radio, Roda has worked his way up the ladder from the early days as "The 20/20 Ticker Dude" to his current slot as the Afternoon Drive Time Host, better known as "Happy Hour" with "The Roadman" every Monday through Friday from 3pm-7pm. Roda, a 1986 graduate of Baldwin-Wallace College, has also worked in TV with then SportsChannel Ohio and now Fox Sports Net Ohio. He served as the Cleveland Lumberjacks rink side reporter during their telecasts as well as the studio host with Michael Reghi for the pre-game and post-game shows covering the Indians during the 1995 World Series. Roda's affiliation with the Tribe is a strong one, having hosted Tribe Talk for the Indians radio network for over 3 years as well as hosting the Tribe's pre-game show for their game broadcasts. Roda also was the host for "10th Inning", a call-in show after Tribe games. The Roadman's knowledge doesn't end with baseball though. He covers the Browns on a weekly basis during the season. The only talk show host in C-Town to have been at every home game to get after game interviews since the Browns returned to the NFL in 1999. An avid basketball fan, Roda admits, as painful as it is he watches all Cavs games and follows them religiously. He also provides play-by-play and color commentary for the MAC basketball tournament every March. His combination of sports knowledge, distinctive interviewing skills, entertainment value and love for "The Hotties" has made his show, "Happy Hour", one of the top afternoon drive sports talk shows in the country.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Foley's primary coverage area is Central Ohio, specifically Columbus, where he covers everything from city and county government and politics to sports. Occasionally, he'll work on a national story for NPR. "If a national figure visits the region, we'll often attend to get a sense of how the issues they're promoting affect Ohio residents," he says. "While we're always open to story ideas, they've got to have at least a shred of a tie to Ohio." A recent pitch turned into a story revolved around actor John Ratzenberger's appearance in Columbus to speak about manufacturing. "I've done interviews with Cirque Du Soleil, because they've had a few shows here," he says. "Musicians are options too, as we have a few locally produced music shows, which feature live performances from local and national acts that come to town." Send him the information. Try to avoid constant follow-ups. "It really comes down to the story idea," he says. "If the idea has some relevance to Ohio, I thoroughly consider it regardless of the pitch." Contact him via email.

Contact Profile: Greg is the lead reporter for WTAM. From wading through ankle deep water while covering a massive water main break in downtown Cleveland to keeping our listeners informed on the corruption trial of Rep. James Traficant to stepping through the debris of devastating tornado, Greg Saber has done it all. The native Clevelander graduated from Kent State University with degrees in Communications and Political Science. Before joining the news staff at WTAM 1100, Greg worked as a reporter at WAKR-AM and WAKC-TV, both in Akron. Greg finds working in the news business a day-to-day educational experience. His hobbies include amateur radio, jogging in all kinds of weather, American politics, video production and trying to figure-out whether he needs HBO on his cable bill. His most memorable broadcast moments include forgetting what city he was in while doing his first newscast in Akron, dropping his glasses from the Goodyear Blimp and interviewing people at Hopkins Airport who had just flown over the burning World Trade Center in New York. Greg has been given Best Reporter awards from both the Ohio Associated Press and the Society of Professional Journalists
